lines.text: Welcome back. [pause] The clock reads {{CLOCK}}. We are {{ELAPSED}} into the night.
I want you to find a comfortable position. If you're sitting, feel the chair. If you're standing, feel your feet.
Breathe in through your nose. [pause] Hold for four. [pause] Release through your mouth.
Again. In. [pause] Hold. [pause] Release.
The Atwood Avenue listeners — I'm speaking to you now. You barricaded early. You did the right thing.
Willy Street. Tenney Park. Near East. I can feel you listening. Your pulse is still elevated. That's normal.
Breathe in. [pause] The ones outside are not your responsibility.
They made their choice. You made yours. Your choice was to stay. To listen. To survive.
Feel your shoulders. Are they tense? Release them down. [pause] Lower. [pause] There.
Now I want you to picture the lake. Mendota. The water is dark at this hour.
The water doesn't ask questions. It doesn't judge the people who cross it.
Some people cross. Some people don't come back. [pause] That's not your responsibility either.
Breathe in. [pause] Imagine the fog off the water. It's cold. It's moving.
The fog doesn't know what time it is. It doesn't know how much longer the night will last.
Neither do you. And that's okay. You only have to survive this hour. Just this hour.
Release. [pause] Release everything you thought would happen tonight. It already happened or it didn't.
Your neighbor three blocks east — do you know what he's doing right now? [pause] No. And you don't need to.
Atwood Avenue spiked early. The data said so. The fire lights confirmed it. You saw that or you didn't.
Breathe. [pause] What you saw doesn't live in your body unless you let it.
Feel your hands. Are they fists? Open them. [pause] Let the tension spill out onto the floor.
The Sisters teach that holding onto fear is just borrowing pain from tomorrow.
Tomorrow hasn't come yet. Right now is still safe. Right now you are still listening.
Breathe in. [pause] Ka-meshta-vela-kesh. [pause] Breathe out.
There are sounds outside that you might hear. Sirens. Voices. The wind off the lake.
Those sounds are not for you. They are traveling to people who are not you.
Your sound right now is the breath. In. [pause] Out. In. [pause] Out.
I've been here before. In the hours between the dark and the dawn. The city looks different then.
The city looks like it's holding its breath. Like it knows what's coming and it's not ready.
But you are ready. You've already decided. You're still here. That's readiness.
Scan your body now. Start at the top. Your forehead — is it creased? Smooth it. [pause] Feel the skin relax.
Your jaw. So many people clench their jaws tonight. Not you. Not anymore.
Your chest. The heart inside it has been beating for {{ELAPSED}} now without stopping. It's tired. Acknowledge that.
It will keep beating. Hearts do that. They don't get to quit.
Your stomach. Whatever is in there — fear, coffee, whatever you ate before the night started — it's safe.
Nothing is happening in your stomach right now. Nothing is happening to your body. You are listening.
Your legs. Feel them against whatever is holding you up. The chair. The floor. The bed.
The earth is still under Madison. Under the East Side. Under Atwood. Under Tenney Park.
The earth doesn't participate in the Purge. It just holds us all and asks us to breathe.
Breathe in. [pause] The clock reads {{CLOCK}}. We are {{ELAPSED}} into the night.
Three hours and counting. [pause] That means four hours remain.
Four hours. That's two sleep cycles. That's time enough for the worst to peak and recede.
The worst is usually now. Right now. {{ELAPSED}} in is when the night is most itself.
If you're hearing this, you've survived the peak. Breathe that in. [pause] You've already won.
The Willy Street listeners know this. The barricade held. You held.
The East Side listeners are deeper in the dark, but darkness always ends. It always ends.
There is a light that will come. It will come whether you're ready or not.
So be ready. Breathe now. Breathe deep and hold it and let it all go.
In. [pause] Hold. [pause] Release.
[pause] I'll be here with you until it arrives.

tickerItems.text: Madison cumulative: 139 ({{ELAPSED}} mark). East Side alone: 27 of total. Atwood/Schenk's accounting for 18.
WILLY STREET BARRICADE: Still holding. Zero incursions past 02:45. Volunteer crew rotating positions every 30 minutes.
"The earth doesn't participate. It just asks us to breathe."
TENNEY PARK: Fire Department (volunteer, post-dawn response assumed) will address structure damage at 614 Monroe Street. No immediate action possible.
PurgePet Boarding: 47 animals sheltered. Your companion is warm. Your companion is safe. You made the right choice.
